questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the four symptom clusters of PTSD?

options.text: Intrusion symptoms such as flashbacks and nightmares
Compulsive rituals performed to prevent the trauma from recurring
Negative alterations in cognition and mood

questions.explanation: The four symptom clusters of PTSD are intrusion (flashbacks, nightmares), avoidance, negative alterations in cognition and mood, and alterations in arousal and reactivity. Compulsive rituals are a feature of OCD, not PTSD.

questions.question: Which factor is most likely to PROTECT a person from developing PTSD after a traumatic event?

options.text: Perceiving a high level of life threat during the event
Prior exposure to multiple traumatic events

questions.explanation: Strong social support is a well-established protective factor against PTSD. Prior trauma exposure, pre-existing mental health conditions, and perceived life threat are all risk factors that increase vulnerability to PTSD.
